articles.headline: Chinese memory chipmaker CXMT wins $3 billion memory supply deal with Tencent
articles.summary: ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) signed a long-term supply agreement with Tencent worth over 20 billion yuan, securing a major contract ahead of its stock market debut. This deal significantly impacts global memory supply chains and validates the growing competitiveness of Chinese semiconductor firms in the enterprise market.

articles.headline: Notion shuts down email product as AI agents reshape enterprise workflows; Slack overhauls AI serving stack
articles.summary: Notion is abandoning its email product to focus on AI agent-run email, while Slack is transitioning to a multi-cloud AI serving stack to support richer agent features. These shifts signal a major operational change for businesses adopting AI automation, moving from bolt-on tools to agent-driven infrastructure roadmaps.

articles.headline: Micron's strong earnings validate AI infrastructure boom and stabilize memory market outlook
articles.summary: Micron Technology reported robust earnings, confirming real enterprise demand for high-bandwidth memory and alleviating fears of an AI bubble. This financial stability supports business planning for AI infrastructure investments and suggests sustained demand for memory components in the medium term.
